assertions.reason: `systemctl list-unit-files --type=service` on Node B showed `go-choir-auth.service`, `go-choir-proxy.service`, `go-choir-vmctl.service`, and `go-choir-gateway.service` enabled; explicit sandbox check reported `sandbox_unit_present=no`.

`/etc/caddy/caddy_config` maps `/auth/* -> 127.0.0.1:8081`, `/api/* -> 127.0.0.1:8082`, `/provider/* -> 127.0.0.1:8084`, and root `/` to the frontend file server; external curls returned `200` with auth/proxy/gateway JSON on those routes and `200` HTML at `/`.

`systemctl show` reported `Restart=on-failure` and `RestartUSec=3s` for `go-choir-auth.service`; after `kill -9` on PID `649569`, systemd moved through `activating/auto-restart` and brought the service back with PID `652132` in `4s`, after which `http://127.0.0.1:8081/health` returned `200` with auth JSON.
Node B has `/opt/go-choir` (`drwxr-xr-x 1 root root ... /opt/go-choir`), and `git -C /opt/go-choir remote -v` showed `origin https://github.com/yusefmosiah/go-choir.git` for fetch and push.

External curls to `https://draft.choir-ip.com/auth/health`, `/api/health`, and `/provider/health` all returned `HTTP/2 200`; bodies were `{"status":"ok","service":"auth"}`, `{"status":"ok","service":"proxy"}`, and `{"status":"ok","service":"gateway"}`.

`curl https://draft.choir-ip.com/` returned `HTTP/2 200` with HTML containing `go-choir` and `

go-choir

`.

Internal vmctl health on Node B succeeded with `curl http://127.0.0.1:8083/health -> HTTP/1.1 200 {"status":"ok","service":"vmctl"}`; generated Caddy config contains no vmctl route, and external `curl https://draft.choir-ip.com/vmctl/health` returned `HTTP/2 404`.

summary: Tested 7 deploy-runtime assertions against Node B and draft.choir-ip.com; all 7 passed, including workspace/git checks, systemd/Caddy validation, external health/frontend checks, vmctl internal-only verification, and an auth-service restart-on-failure kill/recovery test.
